Conditions_Visibility: dry and sunny with steady trade winds and a protected west coast

Wetsuit: 3mm shorty or full suit year-round; many divers go rash-guard only Aug-Oct

Currents: Constant northeast trade winds drive a west-flowing current along the island, so diving concentrates on the sheltered leeward coast where it is usually mild. Southern sites like the Jane Sea and Baby Beach Reef can run strong and are dived as drifts when it picks up.
Wetsuit: 3mm shorty or full suit year-round; many divers go rash-guard only Aug-Oct

Currents: The sheltered bays around the island are nearly current-free, which is why so many divers learn here. The offshore pinnacles — Chumphon, Southwest and Sail Rock — can carry moderate current that concentrates the schooling fish.
Wetsuit: 3mm shorty or rash guard year-round; 3mm full if you run cold in Dec-Feb

Marine_Life_Highlights: turtles, moray eels and reef sharks working the west coast while warm-water reef life stays busy
Conditions_Visibility: hot humid weather with cyclone risk building and calm spells between systems
Currents: Most lagoon-side and west-coast sites are calm, but exposed reefs like Rempart Serpent and the passes around Coin de Mire can run moderate to strong with the tide. Boats time slack water on the wrecks, so currents rarely dictate the day.
Difficulty: beginner-friendly, with advanced wrecks and caverns
Wetsuit: 3mm full in summer (Nov-Apr); 5mm in winter (Jun-Sep) when water dips to 23°C

Currents: Tidal currents run north-south across the mid-channel pinnacles and can be brisk at Shark Point, Anemone Reef and the King Cruiser, especially around spring tides. The Racha Islands are gentler, though Racha Noi's exposed tips can pump when the manta action is best.
Wetsuit: 3mm shorty or full suit year-round; water rarely drops below 28°C

Currents: Tidal currents funnel through the Straits of Tiran and sweep the exposed corners of Ras Mohammed, so most signature dives are run as drifts. Sheltered local bays and house reefs stay gentle, which is why the area trains so many new divers.
Wetsuit: 3mm full Jun-Oct; 5mm in spring and autumn; 7mm (or 5mm + hooded vest) Dec-Mar at 22-23C
